one_liner: Generates differentiated NJSLA math practice sets from a teacher's standards list — no more copy-pasting from prep books.
success_story: ADs and teachers can generate a targeted set of 10 practice problems aligned to a specific NJSLA standard in seconds — what used to take 45 minutes of manual prep.

one_liner: Pulls enrollment trend data from BigQuery and drafts the narrative sections of our annual enrollment projection memo — the part that always took half a day.
success_story: Leadership gets a clean first draft of the enrollment narrative based on actual numbers before the board meeting, cutting a half-day task to under an hour.
savings_replacing: Manual narrative writing from raw exports

one_liner: Paste in teacher observation notes and it produces a clean progress summary paragraph formatted for the IEP document.
success_story: Writing quarterly IEP progress reports drops from ~1 hour per student to under 10 minutes — structured summaries from anecdotal notes, instantly.
delivery_type: A prompt or conversation — nothing to hand off
next_steps: Permissions and logins — need to control access
next_steps_notes: PII handling needs to be figured out before this can be shared more broadly.

one_liner: Takes a candidate's resume and our school's hiring criteria and drafts a personalized outreach message — not a form letter.
success_story: Personalized outreach for every candidate without the time cost — open rates improve and we stop sounding like every other charter network.

one_liner: Translates school communications into Spanish and Haitian Creole, with a tone check to make sure formal letters don't read as cold to our families.
success_story: Translated family communications in under 5 minutes — no more English-only letters going to households where Spanish is the primary language.
savings_replacing: Manual translation requests or Google Translate
delivery_type: A prompt or conversation — nothing to hand off

one_liner: When Dexter flags a data anomaly, this tool drafts the plain-language explanation we send to school ops staff — turning a technical alert into an actionable message.
success_story: School staff get plain-English anomaly alerts with clear next steps — no more calls to the data team to decode a technical error message.
data_context_other: Dexter alert feed + student/staff aggregate data
next_steps: Accessing data — need to pull data automatically or via API
next_steps_notes: Currently manual — needs automated trigger from Dexter.

one_liner: Paste in raw meeting notes and it returns a clean list of action items, owners, and due dates formatted for our Notion project board.
success_story: Nothing falls through the cracks between the meeting and the follow-up email — structured action items with owners and dates, extracted in under a minute.
delivery_type: A prompt or conversation — nothing to hand off

one_liner: Helps instructional coaches structure post-observation feedback conversations — gives you a draft of the developmental prompt to lead with before you walk into the debrief.
success_story: Coaches enter the post-observation debrief with a tailored opening prompt — focused on growth instead of scrambling for the right question.
delivery_type: A prompt or conversation — nothing to hand off

one_liner: Ingests our program data and a grant RFP and produces a first-draft narrative section — cuts the blank page problem in half.
success_story: Development team spends time refining and fact-checking instead of staring at a blank document — first draft of the program narrative section is ready to review.

one_liner: Pulls chronic absentee data from the sheet and generates personalized family outreach drafts — warm, not bureaucratic, and in the family's preferred language.
success_story: Personalized outreach for every chronically absent student in minutes — calls and texts feel human, not automated, and are in the family's language.

one_liner: Let a school leader ask plain-English questions about their school's data and get a real answer — no SQL, no dashboard navigation required.
success_story: A principal asks 'How many students are below grade level in reading this quarter?' and gets an answer — no data pull request, no waiting.
savings_replacing: Manual data pull requests to the data team
next_steps: Accessing data — need to pull data automatically or via API
Permissions and logins — need to control access

one_liner: Takes our existing job descriptions and modernizes them — removes dated language, tightens the requirements, and adds an equity lens without losing the core role definition.
success_story: Refreshed JD drafts ready in minutes — broader candidate pool, no starting from scratch, equity lens built in.
